title: Thirteen Days
premise: It is October 1962. Soviet nuclear missiles have been discovered in Cuba, just 90 miles from the U.S. coast. As a member of the U.S. Executive Committee, you must navigate a naval blockade, backchannel diplomacy, and military brinkmanship without triggering a nuclear war. Hawks in your own government push for an invasion, while the Soviets test your resolve with provocative moves.
setting: Washington D.C., the White House Situation Room, and the decks of U.S. Navy ships in the Caribbean. The world is on edge, with nuclear-armed bombers on alert and submarines shadowing fleets.
goal: Resolve the crisis without nuclear war: either secure Soviet missile removal via blockade and diplomacy, or survive 13 days without escalation to full conflict. Failure means nuclear exchange or humiliating U.S. retreat.
gm_notes: Introduce time pressure (daily updates). Use real historical events as complications: a U-2 spy plane gets shot down, a Soviet submarine nearly fires a nuclear torpedo, or a hawkish general orders unauthorized actions. Play up the tension between civilian leaders and the military. The player must balance firmness with restraint.

roles.name: President John F. Kennedy
roles.description: The young President, cautious but determined. You must manage your advisors and project strength without stumbling into war.
roles.private_info: You have a secret backchannel to Soviet ambassador Dobrynin. You know that a U.S. invasion of Cuba would likely trigger a Soviet response in Berlin or a nuclear strike. You are haunted by the Bay of Pigs failure.

roles.name: Attorney General Robert F. Kennedy
roles.description: The President's brother and closest confidant. You are skeptical of military advice and favor a blockade over invasion.
roles.private_info: You have been meeting secretly with Soviet ambassador Dobrynin. You know the President wants a diplomatic off-ramp but needs to appear tough.

npcs.name: General Curtis LeMay
npcs.description: Air Force Chief of Staff, a hardline hawk who advocates immediate bombing of missile sites.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He believes the U.S. should have invaded Cuba during the Bay of Pigs and sees this as a chance to crush communism. He may take unilateral actions if he feels the President is weak.

npcs.name: Ambassador Anatoly Dobrynin
npcs.description: Soviet ambassador to the U.S., urbane and diplomatic, but under pressure from Moscow.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He has been authorized to propose a secret deal: remove missiles from Cuba in exchange for U.S. removal of Jupiter missiles from Turkey. He must not appear to be backing down.

npcs.name: Commander of the USS Essex
npcs.description: The captain of a U.S. aircraft carrier enforcing the blockade.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He is eager to prove naval superiority and may interpret rules of engagement aggressively, risking a clash with Soviet ships.
